name: Charlottetown
summary: Charlottetown is the capital of Prince Edward Island, Canada's tiniest province, and the place where Canada was born at a famous meeting in 1864. It's the home of red dirt roads, warm sandy beaches, fresh lobster, and the storybook world of Anne of Green Gables.
funFacts: Canada itself was first dreamed up in Charlottetown in 1864, when leaders met to talk about joining their colonies into one big country.
The novel Anne of Green Gables was written by Lucy Maud Montgomery, who grew up in PEI; the book has been translated into more than 36 languages.
PEI's beaches and dirt roads glow rust-red because the soil and sandstone are packed with iron oxide, basically the same stuff as rust.
The Confederation Bridge stretches 12.9 kilometres and is the world's longest bridge built across waters that freeze solid every winter.
Prince Edward Island is Canada's smallest province, so small you can drive across it in just a few hours, yet it has its own cute licence plates.
thingsToDo: Walk the grounds of Province House National Historic Site, where Canada's founding meeting happened in 1864, then peek at the Confederation Centre next door.
Catch the Anne of Green Gables musical at the Confederation Centre of the Arts, Canada's longest-running show, perfect for young readers.
Take a 35-minute drive to Cavendish to tour Green Gables Heritage Place, the farmhouse that inspired the famous redhead's adventures.
Splash on Cavendish or Brackley Beach to feel warm Atlantic water and squish your toes into PEI's bright red sand under sandstone cliffs.
Stroll Victoria Row, a car-free brick street with street musicians, ice cream, and patios, then head to Peake's Quay boardwalk for harbour views.
Order fresh PEI lobster, mussels, or a lobster roll at a waterfront restaurant; the island is one of Canada's biggest lobster-fishing places.

localWord.meaning: Hello or greetings in Mi'kmaq, the language of PEI's first people, who call the island Epekwitk meaning 'cradled on the waves'.

images.alt: Boats and red-roofed buildings line the sunny Charlottetown waterfront on Prince Edward Island.
The grand stone Province House in Charlottetown, where Canada was first dreamed up.
The famous green-roofed Green Gables farmhouse from Anne of Green Gables, surrounded by flowers.
The long curving Confederation Bridge stretches across the sea to Prince Edward Island.
PEI's famous red sand beach at Blooming Point meets calm blue ocean water.
